plain_definition: Under the GENIUS Act, the only three categories of entity that may lawfully issue a 'payment stablecoin' in the United States: (1) a subsidiary of an insured depository institution approved to issue payment stablecoins; (2) a 'Federal qualified payment stablecoin issuer' -- broadly, a nonbank entity, or an uninsured national bank, approved by the Office of the Comptroller of the Currency; or (3) a 'State qualified payment stablecoin issuer' approved by a state payment-stablecoin regulator (generally available only to issuers below a statutory size threshold, above which Federal regulation applies). Issuing a payment stablecoin in the US outside one of these three categories is not permitted under the Act.
formal_definition: GENIUS Act (Public Law 119-27) section 2(23) (12 U.S.C. 5901(23)) defines 'permitted payment stablecoin issuer' as a person formed in the United States that is (A) a subsidiary of an insured depository institution approved to issue payment stablecoins under section 5 of the Act; (B) a 'Federal qualified payment stablecoin issuer' as defined in section 2(11); or (C) a 'State qualified payment stablecoin issuer' as defined in section 2(31).
